body:
We need to take all our meanings and describe each meaning with meanings, that are described by meanings.

Meaning, if we use any description for any meaning, we should prefer it to be also constructed from meanings present at our seed data.

So we fully and deeply understand each and every meaning we have in order for our system to function.

We also need to make sure we don't have any left overs for hardcoded constants and values about any language, we should manipulate only meanings in the code.

Also we don't need descriptions of meanings in pure English or any other language. We should use direct references to other meanings to describe the meaning of each meanings. It is ok to have self referential meanings or meanings that are defined as cycle with other meanings.

But each and every meaning should be described as meaning.

That means that for each such meaning we can construct its translation to any language.

We should also have all the details about each word, part of speech and so on.

Each meaning, word and others should have notation, annotation, denotation, connotation all written as meanings, senses, holons, concepts, not actual words in any specific language.

Remember that everything is a link, and based on links, including meanings, types and so on. As holon the link is always divisible and definable, always a part and a whole, even if temporary it is self defined or part of itself, it can be later updated. So there is always links network, not graph.

Ideally we should be able to merge formal types with natural language meaning, so everything is fully and semi formal at the same time, so for example if something is not fully formal, that means it is another variable or range that just needs to be clarified, if required or kept unclear if needed.

So our knowledge base is meta knowledge base, that is the single semantic meta language for everything.

We should prefer mutation of data by code/algorithms, which can also be used as migrations for older versions of seed data.

We should deeply compare our data with all the sources like: wikipedia, wikidata, wiktionary, wordnet, other thesauruses and reverse dictionaries, regular dictionaries and so on.

We should be able to have scripts/algorithms/functions that will be able to source data from these sources, and convert them to our views.

Our views should include meanings, words, types, categories, concepts, groups. We should support all ontologies, taxonomies and so on, by defining roots (meanings, concepts, types) of their systems.

Each and every data item we have in links, should be routed in actual external sources, with references to them, yet if we do any overrides to make it consistent and correct we should clearly mark it.

And we should have pre cached, pre saved as .lino files original responses from outside systems, so we can compare for changes and so on.

We need to compare what we have knowledge wise and compare it to all popular competitors that are exist in the world.

So as the result we will have the deep foundation, that will allow us truly and deeply understand every detail about worlds around us.

This semantic meta language should be enough to be able to deeply describe everything we need to understand about natural and formal languages (like programming languages and others).

Yet, we that should minimum of concepts, as we should prioritize ability of expanding links network on demand (for each task or text), and don't try to fully memoize everything, but all the core concepts should be present, so we don't spend to much time loading data from external sources at startup.

Make sure our vision, requirements and so on are updated, if not up to the description I provided here.

We need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

Please plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
title: Full recursive definition of all meanings (deeper meta-understanding), better semantic meta language as foundation
